# Quantum Key Distribution Network(QKDN) Security Solution
Orchestrating Quantum-Safe Infrastructure at National Scale
The global QKD market is projected to reach $2.58 billion by 2030, growing at 33.78% CAGR. With data breach costs averaging $10.22 million in the US and $4.44 million globally, organizations need scalable quantum-safe infrastructure—not isolated point-to-point links.
[Request a Demo](https://www.qnulabs.com/request-a-demo)[Secure Your Encryption](https://www.qnulabs.com/request-a-demo)
Quantum Key Distribution Networks (QKDN) Explained
[Play](https://www.qnulabs.com/quantum-key-distribution-network-qkdn)
## Quantum Key Distribution Network (QKDN) System for National-Scale Security
Revolutionizing quantum-secure communication with centralized control, intelligent orchestration, and scalable infrastructure for governments and enterprises.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/660f833875f3ae90553f1584/690d9f63a699efddda24b86b_malware.svg)
QKDN is India’s first software-defined quantum network orchestration platform designed for governments and enterprises. It offers centralized control, unmatched operational resilience, and national-scale quantum-safe communication to secure critical digital infrastructure.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/660f833875f3ae90553f1584/690d9e6d54d5003d0f7309ca_algorithm.svg)
Unlike traditional point-to-point QKD setups that demand separate hardware for every connection, QKDN orchestrates hundreds of nodes and controllers from one command center, enabling seamless management across physical, digital, hybrid, and free-space QKD systems.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/660f833875f3ae90553f1584/690d9ff297dc7468b1f06515_server.svg)
Its platform architecture delivers up to 50% cost savings compared to linear deployments. Centralized orchestration removes complexity, enhances scalability, and simplifies expansion as organizations grow their quantum-secure networks across multiple layers and geographies.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/660f833875f3ae90553f1584/690d9dcac5d2f8daec51f534_structure.svg)
Tested across 500 km of Indian Army operations with 99.5% uptime, QKDN integrates AI-driven fault recovery, intelligent automation, and ETSI-compliant standards to maintain a self-healing, sovereign, and future-ready quantum-secure network.
### Key Value Propositions
Orchestrate Hundreds of Quantum Nodes from One Command Center
Support Every QKD Technology from One Platform
Automatically Heal Network Failures Before Anyone Even Notices
Reduce Infrastructure Costs by 50 Percent Through Intelligent Network Topology
Deploy with Defence-Grade Validation and Indian Sovereignty
[Request a Demo](https://www.qnulabs.com/request-a-demo)[Secure Your Encryption](https://www.qnulabs.com/request-a-demo)

## Why QKDN Matters in the Quantum Era?
Urgent quantum readiness: Protect data from harvest-now-decrypt-later threats, meet NIST mandates, and leverage India and China’s quantum advancements for future security.  

**HNDL Threat Active** Adversaries collecting encrypted data for future quantum decryption; Q-Day anticipated by 2035.
**Cyberattack Cost Explosion** Attacks doubled since COVID-19; costs projected to reach $10.5 trillion annually by 2025
**NIST 2028 Mandate** Full PQC adoption required; enterprises need 3-5 years for cryptographic infrastructure migration  

**China's Operational Lead** 12,000km quantum backbone connecting 16 cities demonstrates quantum-safe national networks are strategic imperatives
**India's 500km Milestone -** National Quantum Mission focused quantum_ projects position India at the forefront with validated defence-grade operational reality.

## How Does QKDN Work?
Unified Quantum Network Architecture: Layered design from SDN orchestrator and distributed QKD controllers to physical/digital quantum nodes with ETSI-compliant APIs.
### QKDN Architecture for Large-Scale Quantum Networks
Layer 1
**Network Manager / SDN Orchestrator** Top-level command providing unified network view, policy configuration, cross-controller coordination via ETSI GS QKD 018 (NETCONF/YANG)
Layer 2
**QKD Network Controllers (QKDNC)** Distributed controllers managing node domains, handling registration, key sessions, routing, local fault recovery via ETSI GS QKD 015
Layer 3
**QKD Nodes** Physical/digital/hybrid QKD systems generating quantum-secured keys, reporting telemetry, delivering keys via ETSI GS QKD 014 REST APIs
### QKDN Operational Workflow
Step 1: 
Network Initialization
Administrator accesses system via MFA, performs inventory discovery, configures topology, and generates network maps.  

Step 2: 
Node Registration
Nodes initiate TLS 1.3 connection, authenticate certificates, receive configuration, and start key generation.  

Step 3: 
Quantum Key Generation
Physical QKD uses photon transmission; QKDN combines QRNG and PQC; Hybrid approach integrates both with continuous QBER monitoring.  

Step 4: 
Key Distribution & Storage
Keys are securely stored in the controller's KMM, indexed with metadata, and automated rotations are scheduled.  

Step 5: 
Application Key Request
Applications request keys via REST API, with authentication validated and authorization checked.  

Step 6: 
Intelligent Path Selection
OSPF cost metrics are evaluated, optimal multi-hop relays selected, and paths reserved.  

Step 7: 
Continuous Monitoring
Real-time telemetry feeds AI/ML pattern analysis, dashboard health displays, and triggers alerts.  

Step 8: 
Fault Detection & Recovery
Link failures trigger BITE self-tests, activate alternate paths, and failover occurs within 5 seconds.  

Step 9: 
Security Incident Response
Tamper sensors detect intrusion, immediately delete keys, isolate the node, and alert the SOC.  

Step 10: 
Reporting & Compliance
Scheduled reports are auto-generated, compliance logs maintained, and historical trends visualized.

## Frequently asked questions 
How does QKDN differ from traditional point-to-point QKD systems?
Traditional QKD connects two nodes in isolated pairs requiring separate hardware and manual management. QKDN transforms this into network architecture where hundreds of nodes are orchestrated centrally with intelligent routing—analogous to moving from dial-up modems to internet. Source: [ETSI GS QKD 018](https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_gs/QKD/001_099/018/01.01.01_60/gs_QKD018v010101p.pdf)
What is the maximum distance QKDN can support?
Unlike consumer apps, QVerse offers enterprise-grade admin controls (remote wipe, DLP, audit), quantum-safeQNu Labs deployed QKDN over 500km using hub-and-spoke topology with trusted nodes. Commercial QKD links reach ~150km in fibre; trusted node architecture extends indefinitely. 2026 roadmap targets 2,000km with pre-quantum repeaters for India; satellite free-space enables intercontinental coverage. encryption, and India-based SaaS deployment with local and global compliance.
Is QKDN compatible with existing network infrastructure? 
Yes. QKDN integrates routers, firewalls, VPNs via ETSI GS QKD 014 REST APIs and SKIP protocol. Operates alongside classical traffic without dedicated fibre (hybrid) or uses dark fibre for maximum performance. Compatible with Cisco, Juniper, Palo Alto, and major networking vendors.
How does QKDN protect against quantum computer attacks?
QKDN uses quantum key distribution where eavesdropping attempts disturb quantum states per Heisenberg uncertainty principle—provably secure regardless of attacker's computational power, unlike mathematical encryption (RSA, ECC) vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. Layered with NIST-approved PQC for defense-in-depth. Source: [NIST PQC](https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/post-quantum-cryptography)
What are costs compared to traditional security infrastructure?
QKDN reduces infrastructure costs 50% vs. linear point-to-point topologies. While initial QKD hardware exceeds classical encryptors, 3-year ROI comes from: eliminating emergency cryptographic migration, regulatory compliance cost avoidance, breach prevention (average $4.44M-$10.22M), unified management reducing headcount.
How long does QKDN deployment take?
Yes. Private clouBased on Indian Navy 500km experience: Planning/design (3-4 months), hardware procurement/installation (4-6 months), integration/testing (2-3 months), operational validation (1-2 months). Total: 10-15 months for national-scale; 4-6 months for metro deployments. QNu provides turnkey deployment services.d/on‑premises options are available, especially fordefence or regulated sectors.
What standards and certifications does QKDN comply with?
ETSI GS QKD 002, 004, 008, 014, 015, 018; IETF RFC 6020/7950/6241 (NETCONF/YANG); India TEC 91000:2022 Type Approval; AIMSCS QRNG certification; Common Criteria GS QKD 016; ISO 27001; National Quantum Mission approved.
What happens if a QKD node or controller fails?
Multi-level recovery: Node failure (automated path re-routing <5 seconds), controller failure (active-standby automatic failover), link failure (predictive analytics detect degradation before total failure). Indian Army deployment maintains 99.5% uptime under real-world harsh conditions.
What industries benefit most from QKDN?Government/Defense?
Secure inter-agency communications, classified data protection, military command networks across geographically distributed facilities. **BFSI** : Transaction network protection, trading platforms, inter-branch connectivity facing $5.9M average breach costs. **Telecommunications** : Quantum-safe backbone infrastructure, 5G/6G network protection, secure service provider offerings. **Healthcare** : Patient record protection, telemedicine security, hospital interconnects facing $7.42M average breach costs. **Critical Infrastructure** : SCADA protection, smart grid security, power distribution, oil & gas OT security across dispersed assets.

Quantum Key Distribution Networks (QKDN) Explained
Play

Quantum Key Distribution Network (QKDN) System for National-Scale Security

Revolutionizing quantum-secure communication with centralized control, intelligent orchestration, and scalable infrastructure for governments and enterprises.

QKDN is India’s first software-defined quantum network orchestration platform designed for governments and enterprises. It offers centralized control, unmatched operational resilience, and national-scale quantum-safe communication to secure critical digital infrastructure.
Unlike traditional point-to-point QKD setups that demand separate hardware for every connection, QKDN orchestrates hundreds of nodes and controllers from one command center, enabling seamless management across physical, digital, hybrid, and free-space QKD systems.
Its platform architecture delivers up to 50% cost savings compared to linear deployments. Centralized orchestration removes complexity, enhances scalability, and simplifies expansion as organizations grow their quantum-secure networks across multiple layers and geographies.
Tested across 500 km of Indian Army operations with 99.5% uptime, QKDN integrates AI-driven fault recovery, intelligent automation, and ETSI-compliant standards to maintain a self-healing, sovereign, and future-ready quantum-secure network.

How Does QKDN Work?

Unified Quantum Network Architecture: Layered design from SDN orchestrator and distributed QKD controllers to physical/digital quantum nodes with ETSI-compliant APIs.

QKDN Architecture for Large-Scale Quantum Networks

Layer 1
Network Manager / SDN Orchestrator Top-level command providing unified network view, policy configuration, cross-controller coordination via ETSI GS QKD 018 (NETCONF/YANG)
Layer 2
QKD Network Controllers (QKDNC) Distributed controllers managing node domains, handling registration, key sessions, routing, local fault recovery via ETSI GS QKD 015
Layer 3
QKD Nodes Physical/digital/hybrid QKD systems generating quantum-secured keys, reporting telemetry, delivering keys via ETSI GS QKD 014 REST APIs

QKDN Operational Workflow

Step 1:

Network Initialization
Administrator accesses system via MFA, performs inventory discovery, configures topology, and generates network maps.

Step 2:

Node Registration
Nodes initiate TLS 1.3 connection, authenticate certificates, receive configuration, and start key generation.

Step 3:

Quantum Key Generation
Physical QKD uses photon transmission; QKDN combines QRNG and PQC; Hybrid approach integrates both with continuous QBER monitoring.

Step 4:

Key Distribution & Storage
Keys are securely stored in the controller's KMM, indexed with metadata, and automated rotations are scheduled.

Step 5:

Application Key Request
Applications request keys via REST API, with authentication validated and authorization checked.

Step 6:

Intelligent Path Selection
OSPF cost metrics are evaluated, optimal multi-hop relays selected, and paths reserved.

Step 7:

Continuous Monitoring
Real-time telemetry feeds AI/ML pattern analysis, dashboard health displays, and triggers alerts.

Step 8:

Fault Detection & Recovery
Link failures trigger BITE self-tests, activate alternate paths, and failover occurs within 5 seconds.

Step 9:

Security Incident Response
Tamper sensors detect intrusion, immediately delete keys, isolate the node, and alert the SOC.

Step 10:

Reporting & Compliance
Scheduled reports are auto-generated, compliance logs maintained, and historical trends visualized.

Frequently asked questions

How does QKDN differ from traditional point-to-point QKD systems?

Traditional QKD connects two nodes in isolated pairs requiring separate hardware and manual management. QKDN transforms this into network architecture where hundreds of nodes are orchestrated centrally with intelligent routing—analogous to moving from dial-up modems to internet. Source: ETSI GS QKD 018

What is the maximum distance QKDN can support?

Unlike consumer apps, QVerse offers enterprise-grade admin controls (remote wipe, DLP, audit), quantum-safeQNu Labs deployed QKDN over 500km using hub-and-spoke topology with trusted nodes. Commercial QKD links reach ~150km in fibre; trusted node architecture extends indefinitely. 2026 roadmap targets 2,000km with pre-quantum repeaters for India; satellite free-space enables intercontinental coverage. encryption, and India-based SaaS deployment with local and global compliance.

Is QKDN compatible with existing network infrastructure?

Yes. QKDN integrates routers, firewalls, VPNs via ETSI GS QKD 014 REST APIs and SKIP protocol. Operates alongside classical traffic without dedicated fibre (hybrid) or uses dark fibre for maximum performance. Compatible with Cisco, Juniper, Palo Alto, and major networking vendors.

How does QKDN protect against quantum computer attacks?

QKDN uses quantum key distribution where eavesdropping attempts disturb quantum states per Heisenberg uncertainty principle—provably secure regardless of attacker's computational power, unlike mathematical encryption (RSA, ECC) vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. Layered with NIST-approved PQC for defense-in-depth. Source: NIST PQC

What are costs compared to traditional security infrastructure?

QKDN reduces infrastructure costs 50% vs. linear point-to-point topologies. While initial QKD hardware exceeds classical encryptors, 3-year ROI comes from: eliminating emergency cryptographic migration, regulatory compliance cost avoidance, breach prevention (average $4.44M-$10.22M), unified management reducing headcount.

What standards and certifications does QKDN comply with?

ETSI GS QKD 002, 004, 008, 014, 015, 018; IETF RFC 6020/7950/6241 (NETCONF/YANG); India TEC 91000:2022 Type Approval; AIMSCS QRNG certification; Common Criteria GS QKD 016; ISO 27001; National Quantum Mission approved.

What happens if a QKD node or controller fails?

Multi-level recovery: Node failure (automated path re-routing <5 seconds), controller failure (active-standby automatic failover), link failure (predictive analytics detect degradation before total failure). Indian Army deployment maintains 99.5% uptime under real-world harsh conditions.
